{"units": [{"unit_key":"unit-004","status":"generated","declaration_names":["AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods.lem_cancel_all_even_l1"],"lean_file_body":"open Finset BigOperators

namespace AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods

lemma lem_cancel_all_even_l1 (n d : ℕ) :
    ∑ e : Fin d → ZMod 2, (∑ k : Fin d, toSign (e k)) ^ n
    = ∑ x : Fin n → Fin d, ∑ e : Fin d → ZMod 2, signProduct e x := by
  calc
    ∑ e : Fin d → ZMod 2, (∑ k : Fin d, toSign (e k)) ^ n
        = ∑ e : Fin d → ZMod 2, ∑ x : Fin n → Fin d, ∏ i : Fin n, toSign (e (x i)) := by
      congr; funext e
      rw [Finset.sum_pow]
      congr; funext x
      rw [← Finset.prod_const]
      rfl
    _ = ∑ x : Fin n → Fin d, ∑ e : Fin d → ZMod 2, ∏ i : Fin n, toSign (e (x i)) := by
      rw [Finset.sum_comm]
    _ = ∑ x : Fin n → Fin d, ∑ e : Fin d → ZMod 2, signProduct e x := by
      congr; funext x
      congr; funext e
      rw [signProduct]

end AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods","notes":["Proof uses Finset.sum_pow to expand (∑ toSign (e k))^n as a sum over functions x : Fin n → Fin d of products ∏ toSign (e (x i)). Then Finset.sum_comm swaps the order of summation. Finally unfold signProduct to match the goal."],"used_context":["Finset.sum","Finset.sum_pow","Finset.sum_comm","toSign","signProduct","signProduct_eq_prod_pow"]}]}
